message:
chore(beads): close ft-gy9eo (codec OOM) and ft-xv561 (pattern ReDoS) now that both fixes have landed

Two JSONL records rewritten in `.beads/issues.jsonl`; status on
both flips from open to `closed`. No new issues filed; no
dependency edges changed. Downstream `br sync --flush-only` will
reconcile the DB.

ft-gy9eo -- codec::Pdu::stream_decode OOMs on oversized varbincode container lengths
------------------------------------------------------------------------
Closed by the upstream bounded-varbincode work (bounded_varbincode.rs
hardening plus the regression_oom_crasher test harness) that
landed in sibling commits. The vulnerability was: a peer could
send a varbincode frame declaring a 4GB inner vec, and
`stream_decode` would `reserve(len)` before sanity-checking, tripping
OOM on any modest-memory host. The fix caps declared lengths
against a frame-budget ceiling and returns a decode error well
before allocation. Regression test covers the exact on-wire shape
that reproduced the OOM.

ft-xv561 -- [HIGH] pattern-engine regex construction has no backtrack limit — workspace-local ReDoS via user packs
-----------------------------------------------------------------------
Closed by `fix(patterns): cap regex backtracking at 10M steps`
(070117a0 in this push). Both compile sites in
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs` now route through the
shared `compile_rule_regex` helper which installs a 10M
`backtrack_limit` on `fancy_regex::RegexBuilder`, and a new
regression test locks the behavior in. See that commit for the
full attack analysis.

Both issues were filed as part of the second-pass security
review; closing them here keeps `.beads/issues.jsonl` in sync
with the code that actually ships.

Scope
-----
Single-file JSONL diff: 2 records rewritten (2 added, 2 removed
at the line level because JSONL is one-object-per-line and
status/close-reason updates rewrite the full line).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-gofz5.2","title":"Create v0.1.0 tagged release with honest release notes and downloadable binaries","description":"# Goal
Cut the first real version release with honest release notes.

# Design
1. Version bump: Set version = "0.1.0" in relevant Cargo.toml files
2. Write release notes that honestly categorize:
   - Working: observation pipeline, pattern detection, Robot Mode, FTS search, policy engine, fleet memory, tiered scrollback, BOCPD, session persistence, connector SDK
   - Feature-gated: MCP, distributed mode, semantic search, web API, TUI
   - Partial: tx execution (orchestration framework complete, data plane in progress), mission dispatch
   - Known limitations: resource-intensive build, Rust nightly required, WezTerm backend dependency
3. Trigger release.yml workflow
4. Verify downloadable binaries are produced for Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64

# Acceptance Criteria
1. Git tag v0.1.0 exists with annotated release notes.
2. GitHub Release page has downloadable binaries.
3. Release notes are honest — no feature is listed as working unless it passes end-to-end.
4. CHANGELOG.md updated with v0.1.0 section.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:02.879546Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:04:07.025577Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:04:07.022546Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["release","v0-1-0"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:02.879546Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:15.706256Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-gofz5.3","title":"Add CI cargo-install smoke test that runs on every push to main","description":"# Goal
Add a CI job to .github/workflows/ci.yml that runs cargo install from the repo and verifies the binary works, catching install regressions early.

# Design
- New job in ci.yml: install-smoke
- Matrix: ubuntu-latest (x86_64), macos-latest (arm64)
- Steps: checkout → install Rust nightly → cargo install --path crates/frankenterm → ft --version → ft doctor --json
- Timeout: 30 minutes (the build is large)
- Cache: cargo registry + target dir for speed

# Acceptance Criteria
1. CI job passes on both platforms.
2. Job fails if ft binary doesn't produce valid --version output.
3. Job completes in under 20 minutes with caching.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:11.405247Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:01:54.765078Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:01:54.763741Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","regression","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:11.405247Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.3","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:56:15.917903Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-grz8r","title":"[EPIC] Eliminate verified first-party placeholders and stubs","description":"# Goal
Eliminate every verified first-party placeholder, stub, mock-like runtime branch, and misleading partial implementation surfaced by the 2026-04-02 audit.

Every in-scope item must end in exactly one of two states:
1. Real shipping behavior with tests, metrics, and operator-visible semantics.
2. Explicitly narrowed scope backed by code changes, docs, and guardrails so the code no longer masquerades as implemented behavior.

# Inventory Covered By This Epic
- CLI / operator support: `build_ntm_stub_response`, `chrono_stub_now`, scheduled backup compression fallback.
- Support crates: `frankenterm/env-bootstrap` and `ringlog` bootstrap/logger stubs.
- Client/runtime control plane: `ActiveWorkspaceChanged` TODO, unilateral-message FIXME, `spawn_pane` unimplemented, alt-screen hardcoded false.
- SDK / connectors / search: transparency verification stub, certification integration probe stub, generated Rust SDK transport unimplemented, `CrossEncoderReranker` no-op, `Model2VecEmbedder` stub, FastEmbed sentinel constructor behavior.
- Core runtime / UX: `NullPaneScrollbackAccess`, FTUI placeholder views, recording delta encodings, stale PaneCapabilities stub annotation.
- Rendering / windowing: Wayland seat+keyboard TODOs, WGL/macOS/EGL resize gaps, bitmap/WebGPU readback gaps, terminfo raw/cropped image gaps, GUI background/glyph placeholders.
- Mux / SSH: tmux PTY lifecycle gaps, detach unimplemented across domains, SFTP metadata mutation, SSH `Match Exec` evaluation.

# Scope Boundary
Only first-party source is in scope. Test doubles under `#[cfg(test)]`, intentional null objects, unsupported-platform no-op shims, and vendored upstream TODOs are handled only if this program reclassifies them with evidence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:26:38.047585Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:31:29.254960Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:31:29.253673Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["placeholder-remediation","stub-audit"]}
-{"id":"ft-gy9eo","title":"codec::Pdu::stream_decode OOMs on oversized varbincode container lengths","description":"Fuzz target `pdu_stream_decode` found an out-of-memory bug in `codec::Pdu::stream_decode` when varbincode deserialization trusted an attacker-controlled container length and preallocated a huge sequence.

Repro command:
`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-pane4-target-fuzz-codec-dev cargo fuzz run --dev pdu_stream_decode --features codec-fuzz-targets -- artifact /Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/fuzz/artifacts/pdu_stream_decode/oom-5c320deb87390600368019c6ffe451a1e575b82b`

Crasher artifact: `/Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/fuzz/artifacts/pdu_stream_decode/oom-5c320deb87390600368019c6ffe451a1e575b82b`
Base64: `DkQEAAQA/////3FxcTBxcf4=`
Hex: `0E 44 04 00 04 00 FF FF FF FF 71 71 71 30 71 71 FE`

Expected behavior: reject the frame before allocation.
Observed behavior before fix: libFuzzer reported `SUMMARY: libFuzzer: out-of-memory`.

Regression test inlines the 17-byte crasher.","external_ref":"/Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/fuzz/artifacts/pdu_stream_decode/oom-5c320deb87390600368019c6ffe451a1e575b82b","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-h164o","title":"[SEV] mcp_manifest_golden hardcodes 6 shared /tmp/ sqlite paths causing parallel test collisions","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_manifest_golden.rs lines 214, 347, 356, 372, 395, 435 each pass a fixed PathBuf like "/tmp/ft-mcp-manifest-no-db-subset.sqlite3" to capture_manifest(). Tests mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db, mcp_manifest_matches_golden_with_db, mcp_manifest_capture_is_deterministic, mcp_manifest_tool_names_are_unique, mcp_manifest_resource_uris_are_unique, mcp_manifest_tool_input_schemas_are_objects all share these global /tmp paths. Under concurrent agent builds (memory notes 50+ cargo processes, multiple /tmp/ft-<agent>-target dirs running the same test suite in parallel per workspace) two processes hitting the same sqlite3 path race on creation/registration. Even if build_server_with_db does not open the DB today (per the line 346 comment), any change that begins to open/init it turns these into flaky tests. This is exactly the anti-pattern testing-perfect-e2e-integration-tests-with-logging-and-no-mocks flags: hardcoded global temp paths instead of per-test tempdirs. Fix by using tempfile::TempDir or deriving paths from std::thread::current().id() + test name.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:02:16.960828Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:27:55.955576Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:27:55.955407Z","close_reason":"Completed: replaced 6 hardcoded /tmp/ft-mcp-manifest-*.sqlite3 paths with isolated_db_path() helper using tempfile::TempDir. Commit 04dc8d76.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-htf0i","title":"[HIGH] GUI resize increment saturation can overflow backend min-size arithmetic","description":"Commit 193a9ebb changes `ResizeIncrementCalculator` to saturate `base_width`/`base_height` at the u16 ceiling (`crates/frankenterm-gui/src/resize_increment_calculator.rs:15-35`). Multiple window backends then immediately compute `base + increment` in u16 without guarding overflow (`frankenterm/window/src/os/macos/window.rs:1410-1412`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/x11/window.rs:1892-1893`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/wayland/window.rs:915-916`). With large padding/border/tab-bar geometry, the new saturation can produce `base_width == u16::MAX`, so `base_width + x` overflows before min-size clamping. This turns an intended overflow hardening change into a runtime panic/wraparound path on oversized layouts. Expected fix: clamp stored base dimensions to `u16::MAX - increment` (or widen backend arithmetic) and add a regression test that asserts `base + increment` stays representable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:57:00.591671Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:45:12.138080Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:45:12.137486Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-ibtjf","title":"[gh-52] build.rs must populate FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_REV for path-dep workspace layout","description":"## Problem (GitHub #52)

After the fail-closed guard from #44, `ft doctor` unconditionally reports `[ERR] WezTerm vendored - vendored commit not recorded` on every clean-machine `cargo install --git ...` build. Root cause:

- `crates/frankenterm-core/build.rs` only extracts vendored commit from `Cargo.lock` entries whose `source = "git+...#<hash>"` fragment carries a commit.
- The default workspace layout declares `mux` and `wezterm-term` as **path dependencies** (`frankenterm/mux`, `frankenterm/term`), so Cargo does NOT emit a `source` field for them.
- `select_vendored_metadata` returns `(None, None, None)`, no `cargo:rustc-env=FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_REV=...` is emitted, `option_env!("FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_REV")` resolves to `None`, and `vendored.rs` reports `Incompatible` at runtime.

Runtime behavior is safe (ft correctly falls back to CLI backend) but the permanent `[ERR]` on default builds is a UX regression that hides real incompatibilities.

## Fix direction

In `crates/frankenterm-core/build.rs`, add a path-dep fallback branch to `select_vendored_metadata`:

1. If `mux.source.is_none()` (path dep), resolve the vendored subdir (`frankenterm/mux` relative to workspace root) and run `git -C <that-dir> rev-parse HEAD` to populate the commit.
2. Handle the case where the workspace is not a git checkout (e.g. cargo install tarball) — in that case, either fall back to a deterministic build-time hash (e.g. `sha256` of the mux crate source) or treat as "local-path-build" sentinel so vendored.rs can accept it without [ERR].
3. Add `cargo:rerun-if-changed=` entries for `frankenterm/mux/Cargo.toml` and `frankenterm/term/Cargo.toml` so the metadata refreshes on vendored bumps.
4. Emit `FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_VERSION` + `FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_SOURCE` in the path-dep branch too.

## Acceptance criteria

1. `cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm.git --bin ft frankenterm && ft doctor` no longer produces `[ERR] WezTerm vendored - vendored commit not recorded`.
2. `ft doctor` reports a real `[OK]` or a meaningful vendored mismatch when the path-dep tree has drifted.
3. Runtime vendored compatibility guard (`vendored.rs:155-166`) continues to fail-closed for truly-missing metadata — path-dep fallback must not weaken it.
4. Build script handles non-git workspaces (cargo-install tarball extraction) without panicking.
5. Unit test or build-script harness covers: git-path-dep / non-git-path-dep / git+hash / missing-both.

## Reporter

GitHub user JordanChoo — https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm/issues/52","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:27:51.238506Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T02:50:52.805074Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T02:50:52.804717Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 3174399d (build.rs path-dep fallback + non-git sentinel + 8-test harness)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","title":"Assemble the final closure bundle, remaining-risk register, and release-candidate checklist","description":"# Goal
Package the finish-line work into one auditable closure bundle.

This task exists because the final state of the project should be easy to evaluate later without reconstructing the whole campaign from chat logs or scattered issue comments.","design":"# Design
1. Collect the evidence matrix, release gates, acceptance artifacts, docs updates, and major closure comments into one bead-native bundle.
2. Record any remaining risks or explicitly deferred work so the release candidate is honest.
3. Define the release-candidate checklist that future maintainers can reuse.
4. Ensure the bundle points back to the beads that generated the evidence instead of duplicating everything.","acceptance_criteria":"1. A future maintainer can evaluate the finish-line campaign from this bundle without reopening the old markdown plan.
2. Remaining risks or deferrals are explicit.
3. The release-candidate checklist is concrete and reusable.
4. The bundle serves as the final closure reference for this whole program.
5. The bundle indexes the unit, integration, and E2E scripts, remote `rch` commands, artifact locations, diagnostic surfaces, and first-run or recovery pathways generated by the program instead of summarizing them vaguely.","notes":"This is the capstone bead. It should read like the final answer to whether the project is actually over the goal line.

Its bundle must index the canonical shared contract in `docs/ft-xbnl0-verification-contract.md` before it summarizes downstream evidence.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod3","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:33.624570Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","updated_at":"2026-04-19T21:05:02.429361Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T21:05:02.429066Z","close_reason":"Final closure bundle, metadata, and completion-evidence docs added","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["closure","evidence","goal-line","release-readiness"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:46.535721Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:22:33.624570Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:46.988053Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:47.443110Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:47.926843Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:23:48.382488Z","created_by":"IndigoBear","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:11.253093Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:08:13.693797Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","title":"Expose live health, leak, and backpressure diagnostics through doctor, status, and robot surfaces","description":"# Goal
Make long-haul swarm health visible to operators and other agents without requiring ad hoc tracing sessions or profiler archaeology.

This task exists because instrumentation only helps users once the important signals are surfaced through the product in a stable, interpretable way. A polished finish line needs truthful, actionable health diagnostics for day-2 operations, not just internal counters buried in logs.","design":"# Design
1. Surface the signals created by instrumentation and release-gate work through operator-facing commands and machine-facing endpoints.
2. Present both concise human summaries and structured machine-readable output so humans and agents share the same truth.
3. Include the counters and state that matter for long-haul swarm operation: pane, tab, or session counts, reconnect or shutdown anomalies, backpressure or queue pressure, leak-suspicion signals, and guidance on when to escalate.
4. Back the surface with comprehensive unit coverage and at least one logged incident-style E2E scenario.
5. Keep the semantics stable enough that playbooks, docs, and acceptance scenarios can depend on them.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Operators can inspect key long-haul health signals without attaching a profiler or reading raw logs.
2. Human and machine surfaces expose the same authoritative health state with clear field semantics.
3. Comprehensive unit tests cover aggregation, thresholding, serialization, and degraded-state handling for the new diagnostics surfaces.
4. At least one deterministic E2E or incident-rehearsal script exercises normal and degraded paths with detailed timestamped logs and stored artifacts.
5. Playbooks, docs, and operator acceptance scenarios are updated to use these surfaces as the standard diagnostic entry point.","notes":"This bead turns internal reliability work into something operators can actually use when a swarm starts to misbehave under long-haul load.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:37:15.364320Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-06T00:01:12.386236Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T00:01:12.386032Z","close_reason":"Implemented HealthDiagnosticsData, HealthLeakRiskData, HealthGuidance types in robot_types.rs with from_health_snapshot() builder. Added Health variant to ApiSurface enum. 21 comprehensive unit tests covering all health levels (green/yellow/red/black), backpressure tiers, fleet pressure tiers, crash loops, leak risk fields, lock contention, guidance generation, serde roundtrip, and envelope roundtrip. All 51 robot_api_contracts + health diagnostics tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","e2e-tests","goal-line","operator","reliability","robot-mode","test-coverage","test-logging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:06.720102Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:41:08.319158Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-xbnl0.5.6","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:37:15.364320Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-xbnl0.5.7","title":"Polish first-run setup, environment validation, and recovery entrypoints for operators","description":"# Goal
Make the project pleasant and trustworthy from the first real operator interaction: initial setup, environment validation, first successful control-plane run, and entry into recovery workflows.

This task exists because `ft-xbnl0.5.4` proves fresh-install and recovery scenarios, but the plan did not yet contain a dedicated implementation bead that improves those entrypoints themselves. A polished finish line should not merely detect that setup and recovery are rough; it should explicitly make them operator-friendly.","design":"# Design
1. Improve the first-run path so operators can get from clone or install to a validated, ready-to-use FrankenTerm workspace without reading source code or guessing about prerequisites.
2. Make environment validation and doctor output actionable, including missing dependency diagnosis, configuration bootstrap issues, restore-state problems, and recovery entrypoints.
3. Ensure the first-run and recovery surfaces point naturally into the new diagnostic, playbook, and acceptance tooling instead of acting like separate subsystems.
4. Back the flow with comprehensive unit coverage for setup or validation logic plus deterministic E2E scenarios that exercise clean-start, broken-environment, and recovery-entry cases with detailed logs and stored artifacts.
5. Keep the result honest: if some setup or recovery path is intentionally manual, document it clearly and make the product surface say so directly.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Operators can move from clean environment to validated first successful run using a documented and product-supported path rather than tribal knowledge.
2. Missing prerequisites, bad config, restore-state issues, and other common startup failures produce actionable doctor or status output with clear next steps.
3. Recovery entrypoints route operators toward the standard diagnostic and playbook surfaces rather than leaving them in ad hoc failure states.
4. Comprehensive unit tests cover setup-state detection, degraded-environment handling, and operator-facing error classification for the touched surfaces.
5. -{"id":"ft-xv561","title":"[HIGH] pattern-engine regex construction has no backtrack limit — workspace-local ReDoS via user packs","description":"Found during /security-audit-for-saas pass #2 (pane 5, 2026-04-21).

## Summary
The pattern engine compiles user-supplied regex patterns via
`fancy_regex::Regex::new(raw)` with NO backtrack limit or match
timeout. An attacker who can drop a YAML/JSON pattern pack into
`~/.config/wa/patterns/` or a repo's `.ft/patterns/` directory can
ship a catastrophic-backtracking regex that hangs `ft watch` the
first time matching pane output triggers the pattern's anchor.

## Evidence
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs:13
  `use fancy_regex::Regex;`
- patterns.rs:512 (RuleDef::validate_regex)
  `Regex::new(regex).map_err(...)` — no `.backtrack_limit(...)`.
- patterns.rs:780 (build_engine_index)
  `let regex = Regex::new(raw).map_err(...)` — same.
- patterns.rs:920 (load_packs_from_config) + :935
  (load_pack_from_id) — pack files are loaded from
  `config.resolved_user_packs_dir()` (default
  `~/.config/wa/patterns/`) AND from `.ft/patterns/` inside the
  resolved workspace root. The latter is an attacker-controlled
  directory when a repo is cloned from an untrusted source.
- mcp.rs:2010 (pattern-engine matching hot path)
  invokes `regex.captures_iter(text)` on every segment for every
  rule whose anchor matched. A single-threaded ReDoS blocks the
  matching worker; fancy_regex's default backtracking engine has
  no internal budget.

## Attack vector
1. attacker commits `.ft/patterns/evil.json` with a rule like
   ```json
   {
     "id": "evil.dos",
     "agent_type": "wezterm",
     "event_type": "noop",
     "anchors": ["a"],
     "regex": "^(a+)+$"
   }
   ```
2. user clones repo and runs `ft watch`.
3. the first pane output containing `"aaa...aaab"` (any near-match
   of the anchor) triggers captures_iter, which enters catastrophic
   backtracking. The pattern worker hangs indefinitely, wedging the
   detection pipeline.

No code execution — DoS only. But `ft watch` is the primary
operator surface; wedging the detection loop silently destroys
the "intelligent detection" claim the README makes.

## Fix direction
Two layers:

1. Compilation-time gate (quick):
   - Replace `Regex::new(raw)` with `fancy_regex::RegexBuilder::new(raw).backtrack_limit(1_000_000).build()`
     at patterns.rs:512 and :780. 1M steps is plenty for any
     legitimate rule and caps worst-case match cost.
   - Propagate the resulting `fancy_regex::Error::RuntimeError` from
     `captures_iter` as a `Detection` of kind `RegexRuntimeError`
     instead of hanging.

2. Match-time budget (robust):
   - Wrap each `regex.captures_iter(text)` call in a time-bounded
     harness (e.g., spawn a short-lived asupersync task with a
     100ms timeout). On timeout, quarantine the offending rule and
     surface a "rule disabled due to catastrophic match cost"
     telemetry event so operators can triage.

## Severity rationale
- HIGH (P1): attacker-controlled repo → DoS on user's machine.
- Not CRITICAL: local exploitation only (repo-clone vector), no
  network reach, no data exfil, no code execution.

## Out of scope for this bead
- RE2/Hyperscan migration (much larger rework).
- Per-rule memory budget (separate concern).

## Related
- ft-security-mcp-path-traversal (30988e53) — same audit pass
  already fixed the MCP arbitrary-file-read vector.

Filed per /security-audit-for-saas pass #2; recommend shipping the
compilation-time gate within one follow-up session.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:04:22.269417Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:04:22.269417Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","patterns","redos","security"]}

 {"id":"ft-y9lnb","title":"[EPIC] Transaction execution data-plane: connect orchestration to real pane operations, policy gates, and agent dispatch","description":"# Goal
Bridge the gap between FrankenTerm's well-engineered tx/mission orchestration framework and actual execution against live panes. Today the framework provides type-safe state machines, idempotency ledgers, observability hooks, and resume semantics — but step execution against panes is synthetic. This epic wires the data plane so that ft tx run actually does things.

# Background (reality check 2026-04-06, updated after three verification passes)
- tx_execution.rs (1,663 lines, 28 tests): StepExecutor trait with 3 methods. SyntheticStepExecutor for testing, PolicyPrepareStepExecutor for real gate evaluation (DONE). TxExecutionEngine<E: StepExecutor> orchestrates full lifecycle but is NOT used by the CLI.
- plan.rs (7,760 lines, 123 tests): Core types solid. Gate evaluation real via TxPreparePolicyAuthorizer/TxPrepareApprovalChecker/TxPrepareTargetLookup traits with StorageBacked impls. StepAction enum (line 529) has SendText, WaitFor, AcquireLock, etc.
- tx_idempotency.rs (2,041 lines, 68 tests): Strong implementation. Integrated in TxExecutionEngine.
- mission_loop.rs (5,291 lines, 116 tests): Real evaluation loop but dispatch is not connected.
- tx_observability.rs (1,287 lines, 20 tests): Event taxonomy complete, integrated in TxExecutionEngine.

# Critical CLI Finding (pass 3)
Both ft tx run AND ft robot tx run import:
  tx_prepare_gate_inputs_allow_all as build_robot_tx_prepare_gate_inputs
This means the CLI BYPASSES all policy gates despite SnowyHorse's PolicyPrepareStepExecutor work. The CLI also doesn't use TxExecutionEngine — it manually calls evaluate_prepare_phase / execute_commit_phase with synthetic inputs. The ft-y9lnb.6 bead addresses this.

# What's Done
- Policy gate wiring (ft-y9lnb.2, closed): prepare-phase traits implemented with StorageBacked adapters.

# What Remains
1. PaneStepExecutor: real execute_steps() and execute_compensations() (ft-y9lnb.1)
2. Mission dispatch: MissionDispatchContract -> WorkflowRunner bridge (ft-y9lnb.3)
3. Timeout/backpressure: per-step/phase/tx timeouts + FleetPressureTier (ft-y9lnb.4)
4. Integration tests: 18 scenarios via TxExecutionEngine<PaneStepExecutor> (ft-y9lnb.5)
5. CLI wiring: ft tx run/rollback use TxExecutionEngine + PaneStepExecutor, add --dry-run (ft-y9lnb.6)

# Acceptance Criteria
1. ft tx run executes real pane operations via PaneStepExecutor, not synthetic stubs.
2. Both human CLI and Robot Mode use real policy gates (no more allow_all import).
3. ft tx rollback sends real compensation commands.
4. ft tx run --dry-run validates permissions without executing (new user safety feature).
5. TxExecutionEngine orchestrates the lifecycle (not manual phase calls in main.rs).
6. Every step produces TxObservabilityEvent audit trail + idempotency ledger entries.
7. 18+ integration tests + 3 E2E scenarios prove the full pipeline.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:12.486800Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:58:22.449058Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T02:58:22.448822Z","close_reason":"All 6 child beads closed: .1 PaneStepExecutor, .2 policy gates, .3 mission dispatch, .4 timeouts/backpressure, .5 integration tests (30 tests), .6 CLI wiring. The tx data-plane is fully wired: ft tx run/rollback uses real PaneStepExecutor with policy gates, timeouts, backpressure, and falls back to SyntheticStepExecutor when backend unavailable.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["data-plane","goal-line","mission","tx"]}
 {"id":"ft-y9lnb.1","title":"Implement PaneStepExecutor: connect tx step execution to Robot Mode send/wait-for primitives","description":"# Goal
Create a PaneStepExecutor that implements the StepExecutor trait by dispatching step operations to actual panes via WeztermInterface.

# Critical Design Constraint: Sync Trait Inside Async Runtime
The StepExecutor trait is SYNC (returns Vec<T>). But the entire CLI runs inside rt.block_on(async { ... }) (main.rs line 16248). WeztermInterface methods are async (return Pin<Box<dyn Future>>). Calling block_on() inside an existing block_on() panics (nested runtime).

## Solution: Approach A (RECOMMENDED) — Make TxExecutionEngine async-capable
Add a parallel async trait:

    pub trait AsyncStepExecutor {
        async fn evaluate_gates(&self, contract: &MissionTxContract, now_ms: i64) -> Vec<TxPrepareGateInput>;
        async fn execute_steps(&self, contract: &MissionTxContract, fail_step: Option<&str>, now_ms: i64) -> Vec<TxCommitStepInput>;
        async fn execute_compensations(&self, commit_report: &TxCommitReport, fail_for_step: Option<&str>, now_ms: i64) -> Vec<TxCompensationStepInput>;
    }

Then either:
- Add TxExecutionEngine::execute_async() that takes AsyncStepExecutor, OR
- Make TxExecutionEngine generic over both sync and async executors via a unified trait with async fn

PaneStepExecutor implements AsyncStepExecutor. It can .await WeztermInterface calls directly since the CLI is already in an async context.

SyntheticStepExecutor and PolicyPrepareStepExecutor keep the sync trait. A blanket impl wraps sync executors into async ones.

## Alternative: Approach B — Use tokio::task::spawn_blocking
Call the sync StepExecutor methods from a blocking thread that CAN create a new runtime:
    let result = tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
        let rt = RuntimeBuilder::current_thread().build().unwrap();
        rt.block_on(async { handle.send_text(pane_id, &text).await })
    }).await;

This works but adds thread-hop overhead per step. Acceptable for the MVP.

## RECOMMENDATION
Start with Approach B (spawn_blocking) for the MVP — it's simpler and doesn't require changing TxExecutionEngine. Then migrate to Approach A in a follow-up bead if performance matters.

# Verified Types (plan.rs)
- StepAction enum (line 529): SendText { pane_id, text, paste_mode }, WaitFor { pane_id, condition, timeout_ms }, AcquireLock, ReleaseLock, StoreData, RunWorkflow, MarkEventHandled, ValidateApproval, NestedPlan, Custom
- TxStep { step_id: TxStepId, ordinal: usize, action: StepAction, description: String }
- TxCommitStepInput { step_id, success: bool, reason_code, error_code: Option, completed_at_ms }
- TxCompensation { for_step_id, action: StepAction }
- TxCompensationStepInput { for_step_id, success, reason_code, error_code: Option, completed_at_ms }

# WeztermInterface methods (verified)
- send_text(pane_id: u64, text: &str) -> WeztermFuture<()>
- send_text_with_options(pane_id, text, no_paste, no_newline) -> WeztermFuture<()>
- get_text(pane_id: u64, escapes: bool) -> WeztermFuture<String>
- list_panes() -> WeztermFuture<Vec<PaneInfo>>

# Design: PaneStepExecutor
## evaluate_gates(): delegate to PolicyPrepareStepExecutor (sync, no I/O, no nesting issue)

## execute_steps(): iterate contract.plan.steps, for each TxStep match on action:
- SendText { pane_id, text, paste_mode } -> spawn_blocking + block_on(handle.send_text(...))
- WaitFor { pane_id, condition, timeout_ms } -> spawn_blocking + block_on(poll get_text until match or timeout)
- StoreData { key, value } -> StorageHandle (sync, no nesting issue)
- AcquireLock/ReleaseLock -> PaneWorkflowLockManager if available
- Unsupported (RunWorkflow, NestedPlan, Custom, etc.) -> success: false, reason_code: "unsupported_action"
- On fail_step match: inject deterministic failure

## execute_compensations(): same dispatch but iterating contract.plan.compensations

# Unit Tests (12 minimum)
1. execute_steps SendText happy path: 3 steps, MockWezterm send_text succeed -> 3 success inputs
2. execute_steps SendText pane not found: send_text error -> failed input
3. execute_steps WaitFor match: get_text returns matching text -> success
4. execute_steps WaitFor timeout: pattern not found in time -> failed input
5. execute_steps StoreData: key/value stored -> success
6. execute_steps unsupported action: RunWorkflow -> failed with unsupported_action
7. execute_steps fail_step injection: forced failure at named step
8. execute_steps mixed: 3 steps, step 2 fails -> step 3 not executed
9. execute_compensations happy: compensation text sent -> success
10. execute_compensations failure: compensation send fails -> failed
11. execute_compensations empty: no compensations -> empty vec
12. evaluate_gates delegates: same results as PolicyPrepareStepExecutor

NOTE ON TESTS: Unit tests use MockWezterm which is NOT async — mock_wezterm_handle() returns synchronous mocks. Tests can use the simpler approach. The spawn_blocking path is only needed in the production code path where real WeztermInterface is genuinely async.

# Structured Logging
- tracing::info! per step: step_id, action variant, pane_id, duration_ms
- tracing::warn! on failure with error context
- tracing::instrument on execute_steps and execute_compensations

# Acceptance Criteria
1. PaneStepExecutor implements all 3 StepExecutor trait methods.
2. evaluate_gates delegates to PolicyPrepareStepExecutor.
3. execute_steps dispatches real WeztermInterface calls for SendText, WaitFor, StoreData.
4. Works when called from inside rt.block_on(async { ... }) (the CLI context).
5. All 12+ unit tests pass.
6. No unsafe code.
7. File: crates/frankenterm-core/src/tx_execution.rs (alongside existing executors).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":240,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:33.101908Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:50:14.230331Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T01:50:14.230100Z","close_reason":"Implemented PaneStepExecutor with 13 passing tests. Dispatches SendText, WaitFor, StoreData, AcquireLock, ReleaseLock, MarkEventHandled, ValidateApproval to real WeztermInterface. Failure boundary stops execution after first failed step. Uses thread::spawn + fresh runtime for sync->async bridge.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["executor","robot-mode","tx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.1","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:33.101908Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-y9lnb.2","title":"Wire pre-commit gates to real Policy Engine: replace hardcoded true with policy.rs authorize() calls","description":"# Goal
Replace the hardcoded-true pre-commit gate inputs in plan.rs (lines 3016-3019) with real calls to the Policy Engine, pane registry, and approval token system.

# Background
The reality check found that mission_tx_prepare_gate_inputs() returns:
    policy_passed: true,
    approval_satisfied: true,
    target_liveness: true,
All three are hardcoded, meaning the prepare phase NEVER rejects a transaction. This defeats the entire safety guarantee that FrankenTerm promises.

The Policy Engine (policy.rs, 14,850 lines + 10 satellite modules) is fully implemented with:
- ActionKind enum with 26+ action types
- PolicyDecision (Allow/Deny/RequireApproval)
- Rate limiting, capability gates, secret redaction
- Approval token validation (approval.rs, 2,356 lines)

All the infrastructure exists — it just isn't wired.

# Design
## policy_passed gate
- For each step in the transaction, call PolicyEngine::authorize() with:
  - actor: the mission initiator identity
  - action: ActionKind matching the step type (SendText, ExecCommand, etc.)
  - target: the pane_id from the step assignment
- Aggregate: ALL steps must return Allow or RequireApproval. Any Deny → gate fails.

## approval_satisfied gate
- If any step returned RequireApproval, check the ApprovalTokenStore for valid tokens matching the (action, pane_id, fingerprint) tuple.
- Gate passes only when every RequireApproval step has a corresponding valid, unexpired approval token.
- Surface missing approvals in the prepare-phase output so operators know exactly what to approve.

## target_liveness gate
- For each unique pane_id in the transaction plan, check the pane registry (ingest.rs PaneRegistry or storage.rs panes table) for:
  - Pane exists in the current mux session
  - Pane was last seen within a configurable staleness threshold (default: 30s)
  - Pane is not in a known-dead state (exited, zombie)
- Gate fails if ANY target pane is missing or stale, with a specific list of which panes are problematic.

# Acceptance Criteria
1. A transaction targeting a pane where policy denies SendText is rejected at prepare phase with decision=Deny and specific step/policy reason.
2. A transaction requiring approval blocks at prepare phase with decision=RequireApproval and lists needed approval tokens.
3. A transaction targeting a dead/missing pane fails at prepare phase with liveness failure and specific pane IDs.
4. All three gates are individually testable with mock policy/registry.
5. Existing plan.rs tests that expect always-pass behavior are updated to use explicit policy allow-all config.
6. At least 12 new tests: 4 per gate covering pass, fail, partial, edge cases.
7. The gate evaluation produces observability events (TxObservabilityEvent) for each gate check result.

# Implementation Notes
- The gate function signature should accept a reference to PolicyEngine, ApprovalTokenStore, and PaneRegistry (or trait abstractions thereof).
- This is a pure logic change in plan.rs — no new files needed.
- The existing 123 plan.rs tests that assume gates pass should be adjusted to explicitly configure policy-allow-all rather than relying on hardcoded true.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SnowyHorse","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:55.327026Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-06T21:42:15.319483Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T21:42:15.319311Z","close_reason":"Already implemented by SnowyHorse. Real policy/approval/target gate traits with StorageBacked impls exist. PolicyPrepareStepExecutor wired in tx_execution.rs. Remote cargo check passed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gates","policy","safety","tx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.2","depends_on_id":"ft-y9lnb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:55.327026Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2912,"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.2","author":"SnowyHorse","text":"Claiming ft-y9lnb.2 after closing wa-2dd4s.5. Next step: trace `mission_tx_prepare_gate_inputs()` in plan.rs and wire the hardcoded gate booleans to the real policy engine, approval path, and pane liveness sources before updating the existing tests.","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:59:31Z"},{"id":2913,"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.2","author":"SnowyHorse","text":"Implemented real tx prepare gates across plan.rs and tx_execution.rs. Policy authorization, approval-token scope checks, target liveness checks, and per-gate observability events are now wired; matching plan and execution tests were added. Remote rch validation is still running, and cargo check for frankenterm-core lib already passed.","created_at":"2026-04-06T19:47:19Z"},{"id":2914,"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.2","author":"SnowyHorse","text":"Implemented the real tx prepare-gate path in  and : prepare reports now carry per-step gate details, approval-blocked prepares have an explicit outcome, and the execution engine emits per-gate observability events. Added storage-backed approval/reservation/pane lookup adapters plus focused gate tests;  is green and the first remote test-binary build is in progress.","created_at":"2026-04-06T19:52:49Z"},{"id":2915,"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.2","author":"SnowyHorse","text":"Follow-up progress: fixed the CLI fallout from the new prepare outcome and repaired stale proptests/scenario expectations. Fresh rch runs are in flight for frankenterm-core test-target compile, workspace-wide cargo check, and cargo fmt check; fmt is green again.","created_at":"2026-04-06T19:55:34Z"},{"id":2916,"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.2","author":"SnowyHorse","text":"Additional validation pass in flight: I narrowed the fresh rch compile to the specific updated test targets (proptest_plan, proptest_plan_ext, tx_e2e_scenario_matrix, chaos_planner_dispatcher) so I can get faster signal on the stale shape fixes while the broader background checks continue.","created_at":"2026-04-06T20:04:50Z"},{"id":2917,"issue_id":"ft-y9lnb.2","author":"SnowyHorse","text":"Fresh remote validation update: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` passed via `rch` (remote exit 0).
